operating_state: Total revenue reached $29.0 billion in Q3 2021, representing 35% year-over-year growth driven by advertising revenue with average price per ad rising 22% and ad delivery volume up 9%. Family of Apps daily active people grew to 2.81 billion while monthly active people reached 3.58 billion, demonstrating continued user base expansion despite moderation in growth rates. Growth was adversely affected by iOS 14 limitations on ad targeting and measurement tools, with future advertising revenue expected to face continued headwinds from regulatory and platform changes.
cost_structure: Total costs and expenses increased 38% with operating margin at 36%, driven primarily by research and development expenses and 28% headcount growth in engineering functions. Reality Labs investments are expected to reduce 2021 operating profit by approximately $10 billion, with adverse financial impact continuing as metaverse development accelerates. Tax provision increased 378% primarily due to elimination of prior year one-time capitalization benefit, with effective tax rate rising to 13% from 4%.
material_events: The company executed share repurchases during nine months with Board authorizing additional $50 billion program in October 2021. Meta paid $650 million in March 2021 to settle the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act class action lawsuit related to facial recognition features. Chief Technology Officer Mike Schroepfer announced his resignation in September 2021, with plans to transition to Senior Fellow role in 2022.
macro_exposures: The company faces material regulatory uncertainty regarding European data transfers, with the Irish Data Protection Commission preliminarily concluding that Standard Contractual Clauses do not achieve GDPR compliance and proposing suspension of EU-to-US user data transfers. Apple's iOS 14 changes have reduced advertising targeting effectiveness and measurement capabilities, negatively impacting advertiser demand and pricing. Over 130 countries tentatively agreed to a 15% minimum tax framework that may increase Meta's global tax obligations.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued adverse impact on advertising revenue growth from iOS and regulatory limitations on ad targeting tools, with Reality Labs investments increasing beyond the $10 billion 2021 impact as metaverse development intensifies. Capital expenditures projected at $29-34 billion for 2022, focused on data centers, servers, office facilities and network infrastructure. The company plans continued headcount expansion across privacy, safety, security, and content review functions.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple ongoing antitrust investigations by FTC, DOJ, and state attorneys general with complaints seeking Instagram and WhatsApp divestiture, with FTC amended complaint filed August 2021. IRS transfer pricing disputes for 2010-2013 tax years could result in up to $9.68 billion additional federal tax liability plus interest and penalties. European privacy regulatory inquiries under GDPR with significant accrued loss contingencies and reasonable possibility of additional material losses. Company became subject to government investigations in September 2021 following allegations and internal document releases by a former employee concerning algorithms, content enforcement, and user well-being practices.
